project.name: bubbletea-zig
project.summary: A Zig-native Bubble Tea rewrite that starts headlessly, renders to terminal today, and now includes protocol-aware terminal input plus editable, tabular, and form-driven UI primitives on top of a shared composable tree.

release.strategy: semantic-release on main creates zig-v* tags and updates docs/changelog; artifact publishing for the Zig runtime is a separate next step.

milestones.name: Core runtime
milestones.details: Single-threaded update loop, deterministic command scheduling, and terminal host live in zig/src/tea.zig.

milestones.name: Headless runtime
milestones.details: A host-agnostic runtime exists in zig/src/headless.zig for automation, tests, and non-terminal adapters.

milestones.name: Composable UI tree
milestones.details: A view tree with rows, columns, boxes, rules, and spacers exists in zig/src/ui.zig.

milestones.name: Reusable components
milestones.details: Spinner, list, badge, progress bar, text input, table, and form components now live in zig/src/components.

milestones.name: Terminal styling
milestones.details: The terminal renderer now respects view-tree tones while headless and WASM renders stay plain.

milestones.name: WASM host exports
milestones.details: A freestanding WASM module with init, resize, key, tick, and render exports exists in zig/src/wasm_showcase.zig.

milestones.name: Advanced terminal input
milestones.details: The decoder and terminal host now handle buffered reads, split UTF-8, CSI navigation, bracketed paste, focus reporting, and SGR mouse; Kitty keyboard, clipboard, and layout-aware hit testing are still ahead.

milestones.name: Higher-level app kit
milestones.details: Text input, table, form, shared focus primitives, paste insertion, and list wheel navigation landed; inspectors and richer layout/styling primitives are the next framework layer.

milestones.name: Browser renderer
milestones.details: The core is WASM-ready, but a DOM/SVG/Canvas renderer has not been built yet.

verification.purpose: Run Zig unit tests across the runtime, UI tree, and components.
Verify the freestanding WASM module is emitted successfully.

verification.command: cd zig && printf 'q' | zig build run | sed -n '1,12p'
verification.purpose: Smoke-test the CLI host in non-interactive mode.
Verify generated docs, status blocks, and markdown tables of contents are current.

next: Finish protocol-heavy terminal features such as Kitty keyboard support, clipboard integration, and layout-aware mouse hit testing.
Move from line-diff rendering to a cell buffer so short updates and cursor motion are cheaper.
Add first-class framework components for inspectors, validation, and richer layout/styling primitives on top of the new form/focus layer.
Add a browser host that renders the same view tree from WASM-backed state.
